examples.input: If \( x(n) = u(n+2) - u(n-2) \), how many nonzero values does the sequence \( x(2n) \) contain?

The input-output equation of the system is \( y(t) = \cos[x(t)] \). Which of the following categories does this system belong to? Please select the correct answers from the options below:  
Nonlinear, Non-invertible, Causal, Noncausal
What is the range of values of s for which the integral \int_{-\infty}^{\infty}sin|t|·e^{-st}dt is convergent?

Which of the following measures can improve cache hit rate?
I. Increasing associativity
II. Using a victim cache to store recently evicted blocks
III. Enhancing program memory access locality through compiler optimization

Which of the following programming techniques and data structures are suitable for use in a virtual paged memory system?
I. Stack
II. Symbol table indexed by hash function
III. Sequential search
IV. Binary search
V. Pure code
VI. Vector operations
VII. Indirect addressing
VIII. Matrix operations
The postfix expression for the infix expression a*(b+c)-d is ______
If a complete binary tree has 100 nodes, what is the degree of the 60th node?

Which of the following statements about the Belady phenomenon and the working set are correct?
I. The First-In-First-Out (FIFO) page replacement algorithm can exhibit the Belady phenomenon.
II. The Least Recently Used (LRU) page replacement algorithm can exhibit the Belady phenomenon.
III. To ensure efficient process execution, all pages in its working set need to be present in virtual memory; otherwise, frequent page-in/page-out operations will occur.
IV. To ensure efficient process execution, all pages in its working set need to be present in main memory; otherwise, frequent page-in/page-out operations will occur.

In a byte-addressable computer, each instruction is 16 bits long. A branch instruction, which uses relative addressing, is located at address 3000. The offset value specified within the instruction is -5. What will be the value of the Program Counter (PC) after this instruction is executed?

Which of the following statements about threads are correct?
Ⅰ. When a round-robin scheduling algorithm is used, a process that has 10 user-level threads will be allocated 10 time slices by the system scheduler.
Ⅱ. Threads within the same process share stack space.
Ⅲ. Threads within the same process can execute concurrently, but threads in different processes cannot.
Ⅳ. A thread switch does not cause a process switch.

TCP is a transport layer protocol in the Internet protocol suite. How does TCP handle flow control, and what response does it expect after sending a connection request (SYN)? Please select the correct answer from the following options:

1. Using a fixed-size sliding window protocol, SYN
2. Using the Go-Back-N ARQ protocol, FIN, ACK
3. Using the Stop-and-Wait ARQ protocol, RST
4. Using a variable-size sliding window protocol, SYN, ACK
examples.target: 4. Using a variable-size sliding window protocol, SYN, ACK

examples.input:
Which of the following functional components of an operating system necessarily requires dedicated hardware support?
Ⅰ. Address mapping
Ⅱ. Process scheduling
Ⅲ. Interrupt system
Ⅳ. System call
Consider a Huffman tree with a height of 5. If two characters have already been encoded as '1' and '01', what is the maximum number of additional characters that can be encoded?

examples.target: The tail node of each list

examples.input: Consider a CSMA/CD Ethernet network with a data rate of 10 Mbit/s. The maximum distance between any two stations on the network is 10 km, and the signal propagation speed is 200 m/μs. What is the minimum frame size for this network?

Given a linear list containing n elements, which of the following operations can be implemented more efficiently on an array-based list than on a linked list?

Outputting the indices of all elements equal to a given value x；
Outputting the values of all n elements in order；
Accessing the value of the i-th element (where 1 ≤ i ≤ n)；
Swapping the values of the first and second elements.
examples.target: Accessing the value of the i-th element (where 1 ≤ i ≤ n)

examples.input: Given that [x/2]{\mathrm{complement}}=C6H and the computer's word length is 8 bits, find the representation of [x/4]{\mathrm{complement}}.

A disk partition has a capacity of 10 GB. Its space is allocated in clusters, with each cluster being 4 KB in size. If a bitmap is used to manage the free space on this partition—where one bit indicates the allocation status of a single cluster—how many clusters are required to store the bitmap?

Suppose node k is a node in an inorder-threaded binary tree that has a left child and is not the root. Which of the following is the immediate predecessor of k in the inorder sequence?

The node reached by following the left child pointers from k's left child all the way down.
The node reached by following the right child pointers from k's left child all the way down.
The node indicated by the left thread of k (which points to the inorder predecessor).
The node reached by following the right child pointers from the left child of k's parent all the way down.
examples.target: The node reached by following the right child pointers from k's left child all the way down.

examples.input:
Which of the following fields in an IP packet header remains unchanged during transit?

I. Total Length
II. Header Checksum
III. Time to Live (TTL)
IV. Source IP Address

Consider a disk drive with 10 surfaces, 100 tracks per surface, and 32 sectors per track. The size of a physical block is 2 sectors, and allocation is performed in units of blocks. If a bitmap is used to manage the disk's free space, what is the required size of the bitmap?

Consider an Ethernet network with a length of 1 km and a data rate of 10 Mbit/s. The signal propagation speed is 200 m/μs. Data frames are 256 bits in total, which includes 64 bits of overhead for the header, checksum, etc. After a data frame is successfully received, the first time slot is reserved for the receiver to send a 64-bit acknowledgement (ACK) frame. Assuming the network load is very light (i.e., no collisions occur), what is the effective data rate in Mbps? Express your answer to three significant figures.

In a multiprogramming environment, an operating system constantly switches between processes to share the CPU. Which of the following is NOT a direct trigger for the OS to select a new process to run?

A new process is created and enters the ready queue.
The currently running process encounters an error.
The time slice of the currently running process expires.
The currently running process must wait for an event.
examples.target: A new process is created and enters the ready queue.

examples.input:
Consider a system with 10 processes sharing n resource units. Each resource unit allows up to 3 processes to use it simultaneously. Select the correct range for the semaphore's value:

[3n-10, 3n]
[3n-10, n]
[n-10, n]
[n-10/3, n]
A forest consists of four trees with 30, 10, 20, and 5 nodes, respectively. When this forest is converted into a binary tree, how many nodes are in the left subtree of the root of the resulting tree?

Consider a double-word subroutine CALL instruction that uses direct addressing. The first word contains the opcode and addressing mode information, while the second word is the target address, 5000H. Suppose the following initial state: Program Counter (PC) is 1000H, Stack Pointer (SP) is 0100H, and the stack top content is 1234H. The memory is word-addressable, and the push operation is performed as follows: first (SP) - 1 → SP, then store the data.

After this CALL instruction is executed, what will be the new values of the SP and the stack top content, respectively?

Given that the ASCII code value of the lowercase English letter "a" is 61H, if the letter "g" is stored in a memory cell and even parity is used (assuming the highest bit serves as the parity bit), the hexadecimal number stored in this memory cell is
A set of record keys contains {25, 50, 15, 35, 80, 85, 20, 40, 36, 70}, which consists of 5 sorted lists of length 2. After one merge pass using the merge sort method on this sequence, the result is

Consider an n×n upper triangular matrix A stored in a one-dimensional array B[0, 1, …, n×(n+1)/2 - 1] using column-major order. The first non-zero element a<sub>1,1</sub> is stored at B[0]. For a non-zero element a<sub>i,j</sub> (where 1 ≤ i ≤ n, 1 ≤ j ≤ n) stored at index k in array B, which of the following equations correctly describes the mapping from the matrix indices (i, j) to the array index k?

k = j×(j - 1)/2 + i - 1
k = j×(j + 1)/2 + i
k = i×(i - 1)/2 + j - 1
k = i×(i + 1)/2 + j

A computer system employs a microprogrammed controller with a total of 32 instructions. The common instruction fetch microprogram contains 2 microinstructions, and the microprogram corresponding to each instruction consists of 4 microinstructions on average. The system uses the explicit addressing method (next-address field method) to determine the address of the next microinstruction. What is the minimum number of bits required for the next-address field in the microinstruction?

Given a system with a byte-addressable main memory and a 4-way set-associative cache. The cache has a total of 64 lines, and the main memory block size is 32 bytes. Assuming all numbering starts from 0, what is the cache set number for the main memory block containing byte address 3000?
What is the minimum value of an 8-bit integer, represented in two's complement, that consists of three '1's and five '0's?

For sequential search, assume that the probabilities of a successful search and an unsuccessful search are equal, and the probability of searching for each record is also equal. What is the average search length of sequential search in this case?
A B-tree of order 5 has 53 keys. If every node in the tree holds the minimum possible number of keys, what is its depth?

A sequence of data is sorted using the Shell sort algorithm. If the state of the sequence after the first pass is 9, 1, 4, 13, 7, 8, 20, 23, 15, what is a possible increment (gap) that could have been used for this pass?

A. 2
B. 3
C. 4
D. 5

A communication channel has a data rate of 8000 bps and a one-way propagation delay of 20 ms. To achieve a channel utilization of 50% using a Stop-and-Wait protocol, what is the minimum required frame size?

Which of the following statements about strongly connected graphs is correct?

Ⅰ. A strongly connected graph with n vertices has at least n edges.
Ⅱ. In a strongly connected graph, there is a direct edge from every vertex to every other vertex.
Ⅲ. A complete directed graph is always a strongly connected graph.

Consider a cache-main memory system operating with a 50 MHz clock. To support a cache with a block size of 8 words, the main memory transfers data in a burst of 8 consecutive words at a rate of one word per clock cycle. Each word is 4 bytes. The time required for memory operations is as follows:

Read Operation: 1 cycle to receive the address, 3 cycles of latency, and 8 cycles to transfer the 8 words.
Write Operation: 1 cycle to receive the address, 2 cycles of latency, 8 cycles to transfer the 8 words, and 3 cycles for recovery and writing the Error Correction Code (ECC).
Assuming a workload with 35% read operations and 65% write operations, what is the maximum bandwidth of the memory system?
Given the network 193.100.60.0 and a subnet mask of 255.255.255.192, what is the maximum number of hosts that can be connected to each subnet?

Which of the following statements about ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) is incorrect?

Ⅰ. An ARP request packet is unicast.
Ⅱ. An ARP response packet is unicast.
Ⅲ. If Host 1 on LAN A wants to communicate with Host 2 on LAN B, and Host 1 does not know Host 2's MAC address, Host 1 can resolve the address by sending an ARP packet.

Which of the following statements is incorrect?

I. With a 3-bit frame sequence number, the maximum size of the sending window in a Go-Back-N ARQ protocol is 4.

II. For a sliding window of size n, at most n frames can be sent but not yet acknowledged.

III. In the Go-Back-N protocol, if the sending window has a size of 16, at least 4 bits are required for the sequence number to ensure the protocol functions correctly.

In a graph G with n vertices, if there is more than one minimum spanning tree, which of the following statements is true?

Ⅰ. The number of edges in G must be greater than n-1.
Ⅱ. G must have multiple edges with the minimum weight.
Ⅲ. The costs of the minimum spanning trees are not necessarily equal.

Which of the following IP addresses is in the subnet 86.32.0.0/12?

I. 86.33.224.123
II. 86.79.65.126
III. 86.68.65.216

Consider a computer architecture with a fixed instruction length of 20 bits. The system supports three instruction formats: two-operand, one-operand, and zero-operand. Each operand address field is 6 bits long. The opcode employs a variable-length encoding scheme. Given that m two-operand instructions and n zero-operand instructions are already defined, what is the maximum number of one-operand instructions that can be created?

A processor has 16 registers and a 12-bit word size. Which of the following instructions can be implemented using single-word instructions?

Ⅰ. 4 three-register instructions
Ⅱ. 255 one-register instructions
Ⅲ. 16 zero-register instructions

A computer system uses a 16-bit instruction word length and a fixed-length opcode. Each address field is 5 bits long. Given that 60 two-address instructions have already been defined, what is the maximum number of one-address instructions the system can support?
An 8-bit microcomputer has a 16-bit address bus. Its 32 KB of RAM starts at address 4000H and occupies a contiguous address space. What is the highest address for this RAM?

Three jobs arrive simultaneously with execution times T1, T2, and T3, where T1 < T2 < T3. The system has two CPUs, each of which processes one job at a time. If the system uses the Shortest Job First (SJF) scheduling algorithm, what is the average turnaround time?

In a given network, the maximum segment length is 128 bytes. Sequence numbers are represented by 8 bits, and the maximum lifetime of a segment in the network is 30 seconds. What is the highest data rate that a single TCP connection can achieve?
A 256 MB memory system is constructed from a number of 16M×8bit DRAM chips. What is the total number of address and data pins on one of these DRAM chips?

Consider a TCP connection where the initial slow start threshold (ssthresh) is 8 segments, and the Maximum Segment Size (MSS) is 1 KB. A network timeout occurs when the congestion window (cwnd) reaches 12 segments. Given these conditions, what is the size of the congestion window during the 12th transmission round?
How many distinct binary search trees can be formed from the elements 23, 12, 45, and 36? Of these, how many are AVL trees?

In a binary search tree, which of the following could be the sequence of keys compared when searching for the key 35?

a. 46, 28, 18, 36, 35
b. 18, 36, 28, 46, 35
c. 46, 36, 18, 28, 35
d. 28, 36, 18, 46, 35
A channel is sampled every 1/8 of a second. The transmitted signal has 8 distinct states. What is the maximum data transmission rate?

A circular queue is implemented using an array A[0…m-1]. Its front and rear pointers are front and rear, respectively, where the rear pointer points to the position immediately following the last element. What is the current number of elements in the queue?
What is the total number of nodes in a full N-ary tree that has a width of 27 and a height of 4?

The ASCII message "Goodmorning!" is transmitted over an IEEE 802.3 LAN. When encapsulated into a MAC frame, what is the number of payload bytes in the frame's data field, and how many padding bytes are required?

Consider a file system that employs a multilevel indexing scheme to manage file data. The inode for a file contains 13 address pointers, which are structured as follows: 10 direct pointers, one single indirect pointer, one double indirect pointer, and one triple indirect pointer. Given that the size of a data block is 4 KB and each disk address is 4 bytes, what is the approximate maximum size of a file that this system can support? (Express the answer in terabytes, rounded to the nearest integer).

A system has n identical and mutually exclusive devices. Three concurrent processes have a maximum requirement of 3, 4, and 5 devices, respectively. What is the minimum value of n that ensures the system is free from deadlock?
In a 128 KB, 8-way set-associative cache with a block size of 16 B, what is the tag for the memory address 0x1234567 when loaded into the cache?
In a ternary tree, the number of degree-3 nodes is equal to the number of degree-2 nodes. Given that the tree has 13 leaf nodes, what is the number of nodes with degree 2?

Consider a system where n concurrent processes share m identical resources (mutually exclusive). Each process requires a maximum of w resources. Which of the following scenarios could lead to a deadlock?

m = 2, n = 1, w = 2
m = 2, n = 2, w = 1
m = 4, n = 3, w = 2
m = 4, n = 2, w = 3
What is the time required to write 1KB of data to a hard disk drive with an average seek time of 12ms, a transfer rate of 10MB/s, a controller delay of 2ms, and a rotational speed of 7200 RPM?
Suppose a binary tree is the result of a conversion from a forest. If the forest has n non-terminal nodes, what is the number of nodes in the binary tree that have no right child?

The DMA (Direct Memory Access) method is characterized by the parallel operation of the CPU and an I/O device, and the parallel execution of the data transfer and the main program. Among the methods for information exchange between I/O devices and the host, which of the following is a characteristic of the interrupt-driven I/O method?

The CPU and the device operate in parallel; the data transfer and the main program operate serially.
The CPU and the device operate serially; the data transfer and the main program operate in parallel.
The CPU and the device operate serially; the data transfer and the main program operate serially.
The CPU and the device operate in parallel; the data transfer and the main program operate in parallel.
examples.target: The CPU and the device operate in parallel; the data transfer and the main program operate serially.

examples.input: Given a computer with a clock frequency of 2 GHz and a CPI (Cycles Per Instruction) of 1.2. The instruction count for a program, P, on this computer is 4 × 10⁹. If the total elapsed time to execute program P is 4 seconds, what is the approximate percentage of this total time that constitutes the CPU time for running program P?

Consider a relative-addressing branch instruction that is two bytes long. The first byte is the opcode, and the second byte is the relative offset, represented in two's complement. Whenever the CPU fetches a byte from memory, the Program Counter (PC) is automatically incremented: (PC) + 1 → PC. If the current value of the PC is 2008H, and the target address for the branch is 2001H, what is the value of the second byte of this branch instruction?

A disk has a rotational speed of 10,000 RPM, an average seek time of 6 ms, a transfer rate of 20 MB/s, and a controller delay of 0.2 ms. What is the approximate average time required to read a 4 KB sector?
An instruction set architecture (ISA) features 200 unique instructions. If a fixed-length binary encoding is used for the opcodes, what is the minimum number of bits required?

Which of the following numbers is the smallest?
(2E)_16, (52)_7, (2000)_3, (101001)_2
When using Hamming code to perform error detection and correction on 8-bit data, what is the minimum number of check bits required to correct single-bit errors?

A disk drive has a total of 200 tracks, and its total storage capacity is 1.6 MB (for the purpose of this calculation, assume 1 MB = 10⁶ bytes). The disk's rotational period is 25 ms. Each track is divided into 4 data sectors, with a gap between each adjacent pair of sectors. The time required to traverse each gap is 1.25 ms. What is the maximum data transfer rate when reading from the disk?

An instruction's execution is divided into three stages: fetch, decode, and execute. Each stage requires time t. If 8 consecutive instructions are processed using a conventional scalar pipeline processor, what is the speedup of the pipeline?
For a decoder with 6 address input lines, how many output lines are there for a single-level decoding scheme and a two-level decoding scheme, respectively?
What is the minimum number of pins required for an SRAM chip with a capacity of 512×8 bits, excluding the power and ground pins?

A given computer system has a total of 70 micro-control signals (i.e., micro-commands), which are organized into 6 mutually exclusive groups. These groups contain 8, 11, 3, 16, 7, and 25 micro-commands, respectively. Using the direct field encoding scheme, how many bits are required for the control field of the microinstruction?

A computer has a 32-bit word length and its memory is byte-addressable. If the current value of the Program Counter (PC) is 1000, what will be the value of the PC after a double-word instruction is fetched?

A 64K × 32-bit memory system is to be constructed from 16K × 8-bit memory chips. The memory cells within each chip are addressed consecutively. What is the starting address of the chip that contains the address BFF0H?

During the execution of a program, a CPU performs memory accesses that result in 4600 cache hits and 400 cache misses. If the cache access time is 5 ns and the main memory access time is 25 ns, what is the average memory access time?
A computer has 64 MB of main memory and a word size of 4 bytes. How many bits are required to address a single word in its memory?

If a chip select address of 111 (binary) is used to select a 32K×16 memory chip, what are the starting and ending addresses of this chip within the memory space, respectively?

A. 00000H, 01000H
B. 38000H, 3FFFFH
C. 3800H, 3FFFH
D. 0000H, 0100H

Data of type float is commonly represented in the IEEE 754 single-precision floating-point format. Suppose two float variables, x and y, are stored in 32-bit registers f1 and f2, respectively. If the value in f1 is CC900000H and the value in f2 is B0C00000H, which of the following statements describes the relationship between x and y?

x < y, and they have different signs.
x < y, and they have the same sign.
x > y, and they have the same sign.
x > y, and they have different signs.
examples.target: x < y, and they have the same sign.

examples.input:
Suppose a single-precision floating-point number, a sign-magnitude number, a two's complement number, and an offset binary number all have the 32-bit representation 0xF0000000. Which of the following shows the correct descending order of their actual values?

Offset binary > Two's complement > Sign-magnitude > Floating-point
Offset binary > Sign-magnitude > Two's complement > Floating-point
Floating-point > Sign-magnitude > Two's complement > Offset binary
Floating-point > Offset binary > Two's complement > Sign-magnitude
examples.target: Offset binary > Two's complement > Sign-magnitude > Floating-point

examples.input: Assume a computer's display memory is implemented with DRAM chips. For a display resolution of 1600×1200, a color depth of 24 bits, and a refresh rate of 85 Hz, what is the approximate minimum total memory bandwidth required if 50% of this bandwidth is allocated for screen refresh?
What is the approximate time required to transmit an uncompressed image with a resolution of 640×480 pixels and 65,536 colors, assuming a data transfer rate of 56 kbit/s?
A computer's instruction set contains 101 distinct instructions. If a microprogrammed control method is used, what is the minimum number of microprograms that the control memory must contain?
Assume a 32K×32-bit memory is constructed using 8K×8-bit chips. What is the highest memory address of the chip that contains the address 41F0H?

A computer has a word size of 32 bits and a storage capacity of 1MB. If the computer is word-addressable, what is its addressing range?
A. 0 - 1M
B. 0 - 512KB
C. 0 - 256K
D. 0 - 256KB

A cache with a capacity of 64 blocks uses set-associative mapping. The block size is 128 words, and each set contains 4 blocks. If the main memory consists of 4K blocks and is word-addressable, how many bits are required for the main memory address and the tag, respectively?

Given that the ASCII value for the uppercase letter 'A' is 41H, what is the hexadecimal value stored in a memory cell for the letter 'F' if an even parity scheme is used, with the most significant bit acting as the parity bit?

In a two-level memory system composed of a cache and main memory, the cache access time is 100ns, and the main memory access time is 1000ns. If the effective (average) access time is not to exceed 115% of the cache access time, what is the minimum required cache hit rate? [Note: During memory access, the cache is accessed first. If a miss occurs, the main memory is then accessed.]
A DRAM chip with a capacity of 1024×8 bits uses address multiplexing. What is the minimum number of pins required for this chip, excluding power and ground pins, with two read/write control lines?
When adding two floating-point numbers, one with an exponent of 7 and the other with an exponent of 9, how should the decimal point of the number with the smaller exponent be shifted?

Consider a byte-addressable computer with 20 address lines. The address space from 0x00000 to 0x07FFF is designated for ROM. The remaining address space, from 0x08000 to 0xFFFFF, is to be constructed using 16K x 8-bit RAM chips. How many of these RAM chips are required?
A 64K x 8-bit memory is to be constructed from 16K x 1-bit memory chips. By what factor is it expanded in the word direction and the bit direction, respectively?
What is the 8-bit excess code for the decimal number -0.3125?
Memory is byte-addressable. For the address range from 0x90000 to 0xCFFFF, what is the minimum number of 16K x 8-bit chips required to construct the memory?
An instruction is processed in a three-stage scalar pipeline: Fetch, Decode, and Execute. Each stage has a duration of Δt. How much time is required to execute 10 consecutive instructions?

A floating-point number is represented in a 12-bit format. The exponent is a 4-bit two's complement number with a base of 2. The mantissa is an 8-bit, normalized, two's complement number. What is the largest positive number that can be represented in this format?

A machine has 80 instructions. On average, each instruction is composed of 4 microinstructions. Among these microinstructions, one fetch microinstruction is common to all instructions. Given that the microinstruction length is 32 bits, estimate the required capacity of the control memory (CM).
(Note: The capacity of the control memory should be a power of two.)
In a common (n, k) block code, what is the number of redundant bits?

A synchronous bus has a clock frequency of 100 MHz and a 32-bit width, with multiplexed address and data lines. Transferring an address or a data word takes one clock cycle. If the bus supports burst transfer mode, what is the minimum time required to transfer 128 bits of data in a single main memory write transaction?
If the clock frequency is 120 MHz and it takes 8 clock cycles to transfer a 32-bit word, what is the data transfer rate of the bus system?
A host computer has a direct-mapped cache with a capacity of 256 blocks. To which cache block is the i-th block of main memory mapped?

A memory system with a capacity of 64 KB is byte-addressable. The address range from 4000H to 5FFFH is a ROM area, and the rest is a RAM area. How many 8K × 4-bit SRAM chips are needed to implement the RAM area?

Assume you have four integers represented in 8-bit two's complement: r1 = FEH, r2 = F2H, r3 = 90H, and r4 = F8H. If the result of a multiplication is stored in an 8-bit register, which of the following operations will cause an overflow?

r1 × r2, r2 × r3, r1 × r4, r2 × r4

Assume an instruction uses indexed addressing for an operand. The value in the index register is 007CH, and the formal address given in the instruction is B000H. The content of memory address 007CH is 0124H, and the content of address B000H is C000H. What is the effective address of the operand?

A computer system has a 32-bit word size and a 1 MB, word-addressable main memory. It uses a direct-mapped cache with a capacity of 16 blocks and a block size of 512 B. What is the length of the cache address?

A disk surface has 200 tracks and a total storage capacity of 60 MB. The disk completes one rotation in 25 ms. Each track contains 8 sectors with gaps between them, and the head takes 1.25 ms to pass through each gap. What is the maximum required transfer rate for the disk channel?
What is the minimum number of binary bits required to represent the decimal number -8196 in two's complement?

A computer has a 2-way set-associative cache with a total of 16 blocks (i.e., 2 blocks per set). Each main memory block is 32 bytes, and the memory is byte-addressable.

To which cache set number will the main memory block containing memory address 129 be mapped? (All numbering starts from 0.)

The main memory is byte-addressable, with an address range from 0A4000H to 0CBFFFH. What is the total number of bytes in this memory space? If this memory is to be constructed using 32K × 8-bit memory chips, what is the minimum number of chips required?
A machine has a 64-bit word size and a memory capacity of 128MB. If the memory is word-addressable, what is the number of addressable units?

Consider a memory system with a capacity of 32 words and a 64-bit word size. It is a 4-way (m=4) low-order interleaved memory. The memory cycle time (T) is 200 ns, the data bus width is 64 bits, and the bus cycle time (r) is 50 ns. What is the bandwidth of this memory system?
A disk storage device has a rotational speed of 2400 r/min. The storage capacity of each track is 96 KB. What is the data transfer rate of the disk?
Assume an 8K × 8-bit memory is constructed from 2K × 4-bit chips. What is the minimum address of the chip containing address 081FH?

A disk drive contains 4 platters. The drive is formatted with 2,000 cylinders, 3,000 sectors per track, and 512 bytes per sector. What is the storage capacity of this drive in gigabytes, rounded down? (Assume 1 GB = 10⁹ bytes.)
A cache has an effective capacity of 128KB, a block size of 16B, and is 8-way set-associative. For the byte address 1234567H, what is the value of the tag field?
If a bus transmits 64 bits of data in parallel at a frequency of 66 MHz, what is the bus bandwidth?
If each character is represented by a 16×16 dot matrix, what is the storage capacity of a font library containing 7,500 characters?

In computer architecture, the CPU contains internal registers such as the Program Counter (PC), Memory Data Register (MDR), Instruction Register (IR), and Memory Address Register (MAR). If the CPU is to execute the instruction MOVR_0, #100 (which loads the immediate value 100 into register R0), which of the following operations is performed first?

PC → MAR
100 → R0
PC → IR
100 → MDR

Assume a relative-addressing branch instruction is two bytes long. The first byte is the opcode, and the second is a displacement value represented in two's complement. Whenever the CPU fetches a byte from memory, the Program Counter (PC) is automatically incremented by one ((PC) + 1 → PC).

If the current instruction address is 3008H and the target address is 300FH, what should be the value of the second byte?
If the current instruction address is 300FH and the target address is 3004H, what should be the value of the second byte?

If a dynamic RAM must be refreshed 1.0 times per millisecond, each refresh requires 100ns, and a memory cycle takes 200ns, then the percentage of the total memory operation time occupied by the refresh is ____.
Assume a computer has a 24-bit address bus and a 32-bit data bus. If the main memory is constructed using 512K × 8-bit RAM chips, what is the maximum number of memory chips required?

In a pipelined computer, which of the following types of data dependency occurs in the statements below: Read After Write, Read After Read, Write After Write, or Write After Read?
ADD R1, R2, R3; (R2) + (R3) → R1
ADD R4, R1, R5; (R1) + (R5) → R4

In a computer system, assume that the hard disk performs data input/output with the processor via interrupt-driven I/O. The transfer unit is 16 bits, the transfer rate is 50 KB/s, and the overhead for each transfer (including interrupt handling) is 100 CPU clock cycles. If the processor's clock frequency is 50 MHz, what percentage of the processor's time is occupied by the hard disk data transfer?
What is the result of adding the two numbers 7E5H and 4D3H?
If a memory has a cycle time of 250ns and reads 16 bits per cycle, what is the data transfer rate of the memory?

A workstation employs a processor with a clock frequency f of 15 MHz and a processing rate of 10 MIPS to execute a known instruction mix. Assuming a 1-cycle delay for each memory access, what is the effective CPI of this computer?
In a paged memory management system with a page size of 4 KB, the page table entries are 2, 5, 6, 8, 7, and 11 in sequence. What is the logical address corresponding to the physical address 32773?
For a four-stage pipelined processor, if 15 instructions are fed into the pipeline continuously, what is the total number of instructions completed by the end of the 15th clock cycle?

In an instruction system with an 8-bit instruction word length and 3-bit address fields, the expanding opcode technique is used. If there are two 2-address instructions and 10 zero-address instructions, what is the maximum number of 1-address instructions?
An SRAM chip has a capacity of 1024 × 8 bits. Excluding the power and ground pins, what is the minimum number of pins required for this chip?

A C program runs on a 32-bit machine. The program defines three variables x, y, and z, where x and z are of type int and y is of type short. When x = 127 and y = -9, what are the values of x, y, and z, respectively, after executing the assignment statement z = x + y?

A. x=0000007FH, y=FFF9H, z=00000076H
B. x=0000007FH, y=FFF9H, z=FFFF0076H
C. x=0000007FH, y=FFF7H, z=FFFF0076H
D. x=0000007FH, y=FFF7H, z=00000076H

Data of type `float` is typically represented using the IEEE 754 single-precision floating-point format. If the compiler allocates a `float` variable x to a 32-bit floating-point register FR1, and x = -8.25, what is the content of FR1?

A. C1040000H
B. C1C20000H
C. C1840000H
D. C2420000H
In the CPU's status register, if the Sign Flag (SF) is set to 1, the operation result is ____.
The following are 8-bit excess code machine numbers $[x]_{excess}$. Which of these will result in an overflow when calculating $[-x]_{excess}$: 11111111, 01111111, 00000000, 10000000?
If the instruction word length is 24 bits and the system supports 130 operations, what is the maximum direct addressing range using a two-address format?

Consider a memory hierarchy consisting of main memory and cache. The main memory capacity is 1 MB, and the cache capacity is 16 KB. Each block consists of 8 words, with a word length of 32 bits. The system employs direct mapping. If the main memory address is 35301H and a cache hit occurs, in which cache block is this main memory block located? (Assume cache blocks are 0-indexed).
In a computer display system, if the capacity of the video memory on the display controller is 1 MB, what is the maximum number of distinct colors supported per pixel at a resolution of 800 × 600?
If the decimal number is 137.5, what is its octal equivalent?
Huffman coding is performed on n distinct symbols. If the generated Huffman tree consists of 115 nodes, what is the value of n?

In an arbitrary non-empty balanced binary tree (AVL tree) T1, a node v is deleted to form a balanced binary tree T2, and then v is inserted into T2 to form a balanced binary tree T3. Which of the following statements regarding T1 and T3 is correct?

I. If v is a leaf node in T1, T1 and T3 may differ.
II. If v is not a leaf node in T1, T1 and T3 must differ.
III. If v is not a leaf node in T1, T1 and T3 must be identical.

Consider a hash table HT with a length of 11, initially empty. The hash function is defined as H(key)=key%7, and linear probing is used to resolve collisions. After sequentially inserting the key sequence 87, 40, 30, 6, 11, 22, 98, and 20 into HT, what is the average search length for unsuccessful searches?

Given the main string T=\abaabaabcabaabc\ and the pattern string S=\abaabc\, if the KMP algorithm is used for pattern matching, what is the total number of character comparisons performed until a successful match is found?
Assume there are 120 initial merge runs in external memory. When performing a 12-way merge, how many dummy runs are required to achieve an optimal merge?

Assume a computer employs a triple-channel memory bus with DDR3-1333 memory modules. If the memory bus operating frequency is 1333 MHz and the bus width is 64 bits, what is the approximate total bandwidth of the memory bus?

A device exchanges data with a CPU using interrupt-driven I/O. The CPU operates at a clock frequency of 1 GHz. The data buffer register in the device interface is 32 bits wide, and the device's data transfer rate is 50 KB/s. If the overhead for each interrupt (including interrupt response and processing) is 1000 clock cycles, what is the maximum percentage of CPU time occupied by the device's I/O operations?

Assume a 100 Mb/s LAN uses the CSMA/CD protocol and has a minimum frame length of 128 bytes. Which of the following is the maximum one-way propagation delay between two stations within a collision domain: 20.48 µs, 5.12 µs, 2.56 µs, or 10.24 µs?

If the network 101.200.16.0/20 is divided into 5 subnets, what is the number of assignable IP addresses in the smallest possible subnet?

1022
126
254
510
In a B-tree of order 4 with 15 keys, what is the maximum number of nodes containing keys?
A memory with a capacity of 256 MB is constructed using 4M × 8-bit DRAM chips. What is the total number of address and data pins for this DRAM chip?

A computer has 16 general-purpose registers and uses a 32-bit fixed-length instruction format. The opcode field (including addressing mode bits) is 8 bits long. The source and destination operands of the Store instruction use register direct addressing and base addressing, respectively. If any general-purpose register can be used as the base register and the offset is represented in two's complement, which of the following is the range of values for the offset in the Store instruction: -65536 to +65535, -32767 to +32768, -32768 to +32767, or -65535 to +65536?

If the response and processing time for a device's interrupt request is 100 ns, and the device issues an interrupt request every 400 ns, with a maximum allowable interrupt response latency of 50 ns, what is the minimum percentage of CPU time occupied by the device's I/O during continuous operation?

Host A and Host B communicate using the Go-Back-N (GBN) protocol. Host A has a send window size of 1000, the data frame size is 1000 bytes, and the channel bandwidth is 100 Mbps. Host B acknowledges each received data frame immediately with a short frame (neglecting its transmission delay). If the one-way propagation delay between Host A and Host B is 50 ms, what is the approximate maximum average data transfer rate achievable by Host A?

Stations A, B, and C share a link using CDMA. The chip sequences for A, B, and C are (1, 1, 1, 1), (1, -1, 1, -1), and (1, 1, -1, -1), respectively. If the sequence received by C from the link is (2, 0, 2, 0, 0, -2, 0, -2, 0, 2, 0, 2), what data did C receive from A?

Host A and Host B have established a TCP connection. Host A continuously sends data with an MSS of 1 KB and always has data available for transmission. Upon receiving each data segment, Host B sends an acknowledgment specifying a receive window of 10 KB. If a timeout occurs at time t when Host A's congestion window is 8 KB, what will be the size of Host A's sending window (in KB) after 10 RTTs, assuming no further timeouts occur?
What is the minimum integer that can be represented by an 8-bit two's complement number consisting of three "1"s and five "0"s?

Assume a 32-bit byte-addressable main memory address. Direct mapping is used between main memory and cache. The block size is 4 words, with a word length of 32 bits, and a write-back policy is employed. What is the minimum total size (in bits) of a cache capable of storing 4K words of data?

A file's inode contains 10 direct pointers, one single indirect pointer, and one double indirect pointer. The disk block size is 1 KB, and each index pointer occupies 4 bytes. If the inode for a file is already in memory, how many disk accesses are required to read the disk blocks corresponding to file offsets (byte-addressed) 1234 and 307400 into memory, respectively?

A file system uses a bitmap to manage disk space allocation. The bitmap is stored in disk blocks 32 through 127. The disk block size is 1024 bytes, and both disk blocks and bytes within blocks are indexed starting from 0. If disk block number 409612 is to be released, what are the disk block number and the byte index within that block containing the bit to be modified?

Host A and Host B establish a new TCP connection. Host A's initial congestion threshold is 32 KB. Host A continuously sends data to Host B in segments with an MSS of 1 KB and always has data available for transmission. Host B allocates a 16 KB receive buffer for this connection and acknowledges each data segment, assuming negligible segment transmission delay. If all data received by Host B is stored in the buffer and not retrieved, what is the size of Host A's sending window (in KB) after 4 RTTs have passed since the connection was established, assuming no timeouts occur?

An undirected graph G has 16 edges. It contains 3 vertices of degree 4 and 4 vertices of degree 3. If the degrees of all other vertices are less than 3, what is the minimum number of vertices in graph G?

Assume computers M1 and M2 have the same Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) and clock frequencies of 1.5 GHz and 1.2 GHz, respectively. A benchmark program P is executed on both machines, yielding an average CPI of 2 for M1 and 1 for M2. What is the ratio of the execution time of program P on M1 to that on M2?

A computer is byte-addressable, has a fixed instruction word length, and uses only two instruction formats. There are 29 three-address instructions and 107 two-address instructions. If each address field is 6 bits long, what is the minimum instruction word length?

If the maximum data transfer rate of a channel under noiseless conditions is no less than the maximum data transfer rate with a signal-to-noise ratio of 30 dB, what is the minimum number of signal states?
Consider a 12×12 symmetric matrix M. Its upper triangular elements m_i,j (1≤i≤j≤12) are stored in a one-dimensional C array N in row-major order. What is the index of element m_6,6 in N?
What is the minimum number of keys in a B-tree of order 3 with a height of 5?

Assume the memory array of a DRAM chip has r rows and c columns. For a 2K × 1-bit DRAM chip, what are the respective values of r and c that minimize the number of address pins and minimize the refresh overhead?
